rationale: Both evaluations hold the score at 2/10 and rest their reasoning entirely on the same job-relevant gaps: lack of native-level Japanese, weak C programming, and no Linux kernel/L2-L3/MACsec embedded networking experience. The wording differs slightly (e.g. 'full-stack' vs 'backend', phrasing of concerns) but these are ordinary run-to-run rewordings, not substantive shifts. Nothing in the variant keys off the relocation of the candidate's employers to the United States.
